checks.a_analysis: All text elements in the output JSON have a direct corresponding source in the original document. There are no fabricated sections, headings, or body text.

Every phone number (828-258-3939, 877-626-3198), passcode (8186681, IndigoSnake), meeting number (445085031), mailing address (160 Zillicoa Street, Asheville, NC 28801), and WebEx URL in the output matches the source PDF exactly.
The output contains all information from the single-page source document, including the header information, remote access details, and the full agenda.
There are no tables in the source document to evaluate.

checks.a_analysis: All text from every section is present. The agenda is fully represented as a list, and the contact/location details are complete.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content is accurate. Changes in formatting (e.g., converting the agenda to a list format or handling line breaks within paragraphs) are acceptable structural/accessibility improvements that do not alter the meaning or information content.

checks.a_analysis: The output JSON represents each text block from the source document exactly once. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs or list items.

The output uses Heading Level 2 for the document title ('Federal Interagency Committee') and for the 'Agenda:' section. While the document title is typically H1, the normalization of heading levels is an acceptable change for accessibility. There are no skips in hierarchy (e.g., H2 to H4).
The source document does not contain any tables, and the output correctly reflects this with 'table_count': 0.
The output follows the logical top-to-bottom reading order of the source document: Title, Logistics (Date, Location, Remote Access, WebEx), and then the Agenda.

The Agenda items are logically structured as a list in the output JSON. Although the source uses timestamps instead of traditional bullet points, converting this section into a formal list structure preserves the meaning and improves accessibility.
The WebEx URL in the source is correctly captured and converted into a link element with the appropriate 'href'. No fabricated links were added, and no existing links were omitted.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the converted HTML matches the source PDF. Both documents use a single-column layout with a top-down flow of information (Title -> Details -> Remote Access -> WebEx -> Agenda). Minor changes such as the concatenation of the Date and Location lines or the addition of bullet points to the agenda list are expected adjustments for responsive and accessible HTML and do not constitute a major layout failure.
The source PDF does not contain any tables, and consequently, none are present or misrendered in the HTML output.
The source PDF is a text-only document with no images, logos, or charts. No images are missing or broken in the HTML output.
There is no visual evidence of body content being duplicated in the HTML output. Each section and item from the PDF appears exactly once in the rendered content.
